title: Agent Quality: Evaluation, Logging, and Tracing
summary: Learn how to make sure your agent is working correctly and safely before you share it with others.

concepts.name: Why Evaluation Matters
concepts.explanation: An agent might give wrong answers or do unsafe things without you knowing. Evaluation means testing your agent against known correct answers to check if it is working as expected.

concepts.name: Test Cases and Rubrics
concepts.explanation: A test case is a sample input with a known expected output. A rubric is a checklist of what a good answer looks like. Together they help you measure agent quality automatically.

concepts.name: Logging and Tracing
concepts.explanation: Logging means recording what your agent did and why. Tracing means following the exact path of a request through your system. These tools help you find bugs and understand failures.

concepts.name: The Improvement Loop
concepts.explanation: Good agent teams run evaluations regularly. When an agent fails a test, they fix the instructions or tools and run again. This loop keeps making the agent better over time.

quiz_questions.question: What is the purpose of a test case?
options: A) To make the code look longer
B) To check if the agent gives the right answer to a known input
C) To speed up the agent
D) To connect to the internet
quiz_questions.explanation: Test cases verify that your agent behaves correctly for known inputs.

quiz_questions.question: What does logging help you do?
options: A) Make the UI look better
B) Record what the agent did so you can find bugs later
C) Save money on API calls
quiz_questions.explanation: Logs record agent decisions and actions, which helps you debug and improve the system.

quiz_questions.question: What is the improvement loop?
options: A) A type of for-loop in Python
B) Running evaluations, finding failures, fixing them, and testing again
C) A way to make agents run faster
D) A method to compress data
quiz_questions.explanation: The improvement loop is the continuous cycle of evaluate → fix → evaluate again.
